sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_arrays (id Int32, arr1 Array(Int32), arr2 Array(Int32), nullable_col Nullable(String));
INSERT INTO test_arrays VALUES (1, [1,2,3,4], [2,3], 'test'), (2, [1, NULL, 3], [NULL, 3], NULL), (3, [5,6,7,8], [6,7], 'example');
SELECT id, hasSubstr(arr1, arr2) AS has_substr, nullable_col IS NULL AS is_nullable FROM test_arrays WHERE nullable_col IS NULL;
SELECT id, hasSubstr(arr1, arr2) AS has_substr FROM (SELECT id, arr1, arr2 FROM test_arrays WHERE nullable_col IS NOT NULL) AS subquery HAVING has_substr = 1;
CREATE TABLE test_nulls (id Int32, n Nullable(Int32));
INSERT INTO test_nulls VALUES (1, 100), (2, NULL), (3, 200), (4, NULL);
SELECT id, hasSubstr(arrayMap(x -> IF(n IS NULL, 0, x), groupArray(n)), [0]) AS has_substr FROM test_nulls GROUP BY id;
SELECT id, (SELECT count() FROM test_nulls WHERE n IS NULL) AS null_count, hasSubstr([1,2,3], [2,3]) AS check_array FROM test_nulls HAVING null_count > 0;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_table (id Int32, arr Array(Int32));
SELECT id, hasSubstr(arr, [NULL]) AS has_nulls FROM temp_table WHERE arr[1] IS NULL;
SELECT id, hasSubstr(arr, [3,4]) AS has_substr_test FROM (SELECT * FROM temp_table WHERE hasSubstr(arr, [3,4]) = 1) AS join_test ORDER BY has_substr_test DESC;
